comments.body: FORCE_CLOSE: close gate(s) bypassed by user approval — no completion/signature attestation was signed. Reason: "Merged via Gerrit (change 211).". Session: f70abed86.

compiled_state.description:
# BUG: code-review vote renders a fail-closed DET detector match as "BLOCK — finding / 0 blocking issues"

## Reproduction Steps
1. Push a change touching a file that a fail-closed DET detector (gitleaks secret-detection) matches on.
2. The 4-pass LLM gate returns PASS (no LLM blocking findings), but apply_failclosed forces BLOCK.
3. The Gerrit vote is LLM-Review-1 tagged "[LLM-Review: BLOCK — finding]" with message "rebar code review found 0 blocking issue(s):" — and NO robot comment names the match.

Root cause: src/rebar/llm/code_review/detectors.py:123-151 apply_failclosed sets
verdict["verdict"]="BLOCK" on a match but never appends the match to verdict["blocking"]
(it only records it under coverage.security_detectors). review_bot/adapter.py then counts
len(verdict["blocking"])==0 and renders "found 0 blocking issue(s)"; adapter.py:183-191 tags
it "finding". voter.py posts only the message. So a genuine detector block is unnamed and
non-actionable — and a REAL leaked secret is hidden from the author identically.

Expected: a fail-closed detector match is surfaced as a named, counted blocking finding.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] apply_failclosed appends a kernel-shaped finding (criterion + file + remediation hint) to verdict["blocking"] when blocking_enabled and there is a match; the finalize invariant holds.
- [ ] Gerrit message reads "found N blocking issue(s):" (N>=1) and names the match; review_code CLI/MCP render it too.
- [ ] RED-first regression tests: test_failclosed_forces_block_on_detector_match asserts verdict["blocking"] non-empty + names the criterion; an adapter-seam test asserts the message never reads "found 0".
- [ ] make lint + code-review suites green; commit carries a rebar-ticket trailer.
compiled_state.title: BUG: code-review vote renders a DET detector match as 'BLOCK — finding / 0 blocking issues'
